# Dune CMS — llms.txt

Dune is a Deno-native, flat-file CMS with TypeScript throughout. Content lives in Markdown/MDX/TSX files. The core primitive is the plugin. The data layer uses a unified YAML schema format. Auth is built-in and sessionless-by-default.

Full reference: https://dune.dev/docs
Full llms context: https://dune.dev/llms-full.txt

---

## Project layout

```
my-site/
  site.yaml               # all top-level config
  content/                # Markdown, MDX, TSX pages
    01.home/
      default.md          # → route /
    02.blog/
      default.md          # → route /blog
      01.hello-world.md   # → route /blog/hello-world
    _drafts/              # non-routable (underscore prefix)
  schemas/                # data model definitions (YAML)
    comments.yaml
    subscriptions.yaml
  plugins/                # project plugins
    comments.ts
    newsletter.ts
  jobs/                   # background jobs (auto-registered)
    weekly-digest.ts
  emails/                 # email templates
    welcome.email.tsx
    digest.email.mdx
  themes/                 # rendering layer
    default/
      templates/
        default.tsx
        post.tsx
      static/
        style.css
  data/                   # runtime data (flat files)
    users/
  .dune/                  # runtime scratch (gitignore this)
```

Numeric prefixes on folders/files control sort order and are stripped from routes. `default.md` inside a folder is that folder's index page. Underscore-prefixed folders are non-routable.

---

## site.yaml top-level keys

```yaml
site:
  name: My Site
  url: https://example.com
  homepage: 01.home       # folder whose default.md serves as /

auth:                     # public user auth (optional)
  mode: dune              # or: external-jwt
  userStore: local        # session | local | db
  providers:
    github: { clientId: "...", clientSecret: "..." }
    magicLink: { enabled: true }

content:
  taxonomies: [tags, category]

i18n:
  defaultLanguage: en
  languages: [en, de, fr]

email:
  from: "My Site <hello@example.com>"
  provider: resend        # smtp | postmark | sendgrid
  resend: { apiKey: "${RESEND_API_KEY}" }

db:                       # optional — enables store: db schemas
  url: "${DATABASE_URL}"

system:
  debug: false
  port: 8000
```

---

## Plugin shape

Plugins are the extension primitive. One file per plugin in `plugins/`.

```ts
// plugins/my-plugin.ts
import type { DunePlugin } from "@dune/core";

export default {
  name: "my-plugin",
  version: "1.0.0",

  hooks: {
    onContentLoad: async (ctx, page) => {
      // runs after every page is loaded into the index
      ctx.logger.info(`Loaded: ${page.route}`);
    },

    onPageRender: async (ctx, page) => {
      // runs before a page is rendered; can mutate page
      page.frontmatter.processedAt = new Date().toISOString();
    },

    onAdminRoutes: (ctx, router) => {
      // add custom admin API or UI routes
      router.get("/admin/api/my-plugin/stats", requirePermission("admin"), async (req) => {
        return Response.json({ count: 42 });
      });
    },
  },
} satisfies DunePlugin;
```

Hook context (`ctx`) provides: `ctx.content`, `ctx.email`, `ctx.db`, `ctx.config`, `ctx.logger`.

Admin routes MUST use `requirePermission("admin")` + `csrfCheck()` guards. Both are imported from `@dune/core`.

---

## Auth

Two modes, same `ctx.state.user` output.

**mode: dune** — built-in session stack (OAuth + magic link, no passwords):
```yaml
auth:
  mode: dune
  userStore: local
  providers:
    github: { clientId: "${GITHUB_CLIENT_ID}", clientSecret: "${GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET}" }
    magicLink: { enabled: true }
```

**mode: external-jwt** — validate and trust (Clerk, Auth0, Supabase Auth):
```yaml
auth:
  mode: external-jwt
  jwt:
    issuer: "https://your-app.clerk.accounts.dev"
    jwksUrl: "https://your-app.clerk.accounts.dev/.well-known/jwks.json"
    userClaims: { id: sub, email: email, name: name }
```

`ctx.state.user` is always `null` on unauthenticated requests (never undefined).

Protect a route:
```ts
// routes/_middleware.ts
export async function handler(req: Request, ctx: FreshContext) {
  if (!ctx.state.user) return Response.redirect(new URL("/auth/login", req.url));
  return ctx.next();
}
```

Content gating via frontmatter (no manual middleware needed):
```yaml
---
roles: member             # single group
roles: [member, admin]    # any (OR)
roles:
  all: [member, verified] # all (AND)
---
```

---

## Schemas (data models)

One YAML file per model in `schemas/`. `store:` is required.

```yaml
# schemas/comments.yaml
name: comment
store: local              # flat files — no DB required
fields:
  - name: body
    type: string
    required: true
  - name: pageRoute
    type: string
  - name: author
    type: string
  - name: createdAt
    type: datetime
    default: now
```

Query interface (same shape for local and db):
```ts
import { local } from "@/db";

const comments = await local.comments.find({
  where: { pageRoute: "/blog/hello-world" },
  orderBy: { field: "createdAt", dir: "desc" },
  limit: 20,
});

await local.comments.create({ body: "Nice post!", pageRoute: "/blog/hello-world", author: "Alice" });
```

`store: db` uses `db.*` instead of `local.*`, stores UUIDs, and persists to the configured database.

Run `dune codegen` after adding or changing a schema to regenerate TypeScript types and the `@/db` index.

---

## Background jobs

One file per job in `jobs/`. Name the file after the job. Export `schedule` (cron) and a default handler.

```ts
// jobs/weekly-digest.ts
import type { JobContext } from "@dune/core";

export const schedule = "0 9 * * MON";

export default async function handler(ctx: JobContext) {
  const posts = await ctx.content.find({ type: "post", limit: 5 });
  await ctx.email.send({
    to: "subscribers@example.com",
    subject: "Weekly digest",
    template: "digest",
    data: { posts, siteUrl: ctx.config.site.url },
  });
}
```

Jobs auto-register on startup. No imports or registration needed. View at `/admin/jobs`. Run manually: `dune jobs:run weekly-digest`.

---

## Email

```ts
await email.send({
  to: "user@example.com",
  subject: "Welcome!",
  template: "welcome",    // resolves emails/welcome.email.{tsx,mdx,md}
  data: { name: "Alice" },
});
```

Template formats: `.email.tsx` (typed, `export type Data`), `.email.mdx` (prose-heavy), `.email.md` (`{{key}}` substitution).

In development, email is never sent — written to `.dune/dev-email/` and visible at `/admin/email-preview`.

---

## Content queries

```ts
// In plugins, jobs, TSX templates:
const posts = await ctx.content.find({
  type: "blog",           // folder name with prefix stripped (02.blog/ → "blog")
  where: { draft: false },
  orderBy: "publishedAt",
  limit: 10,
});
```

`type` is the folder name without the numeric prefix. Never include the prefix.

---

## Key gotchas

**Plugin file location**: `plugins/my-plugin.ts` — not `src/plugins/`, not nested subdirectories.

**Admin route guards are not optional.** Every admin route must call `requirePermission("admin")` and `csrfCheck()`. There is no default protection.

**Numeric prefix is stripped from routes.** `01.blog/01.hello-world.md` → `/blog/hello-world`. Never construct URLs using the prefix.

**`default.md` is the folder index.** A file named `default.md` inside a folder maps to that folder's route. `index.md` is not special.

**`type` in content queries is the stripped folder name.** Posts in `02.blog/` are `type: "blog"`. Never include the numeric prefix.

**`store:` is required in schemas.** Omitting it is a validation error. Choose `local` (no DB) or `db` (requires `db:` config).

**`local.*` vs `db.*` query interfaces.** `store: local` schemas use `local.comments.*`; `store: db` schemas use `db.comments.*`. Mixing them causes a runtime error.

**Run `dune codegen` after schema changes.** Types and the `@/db` import are generated — they're not inferred at runtime.

**Email `template:` is the base name only.** `template: "welcome"` not `"emails/welcome.email.tsx"`.

**`ctx.state.user` is `null`, not `undefined`.**

**TSX content pages run with full Deno permissions.** Gate `.tsx` format to trusted authors only.

**`draft: true` is not a security gate.** Use `roles:` frontmatter for access control. Draft only controls index visibility.

---

## Common tasks

| Task | Where to look |
|------|---------------|
| Add login/logout | dune-auth skill / auth config in site.yaml |
| Gate content by role | Add `roles:` to frontmatter |
| Check permission in code | `authz.check()` — dune-authz skill |
| Define a data model | schemas/*.yaml — dune-schemas skill |
| Write a plugin | plugins/*.ts — dune-plugin-authoring skill |
| Schedule a recurring task | jobs/*.ts — dune-jobs skill |
| Send email | `email.send()` — dune-email skill |
| Create content | content/**/*.{md,mdx,tsx} — dune-content skill |
| Query live engine from Claude | `dune mcp:serve` — dune-mcp skill |
| Mutate content from agent | `POST /admin/api/dev/apply` (dev mode only) |
| Read current page source | `GET /admin/api/page-source?route=/blog/post` |
| Preview rendered output | `POST /admin/api/render-markdown` |
| Scaffold new page | `dune content:create /blog/my-post` |
| Discover frontmatter schema | `dune blueprint:show <template> --json` |

Skills are installed in `.claude/skills/` — read them for code-complete domain reference.
Run `dune update:skills` after upgrading to get updated skill files.

---

## Agent integration

**MCP server** — start with `dune mcp:serve` (or via `.mcp.json`). Exposes tools:
- `list_pages`, `get_page`, `search_content`, `get_taxonomy`
- `get_config`, `get_runtime_info`, `list_templates`

And resources: `dune://site/config`, `dune://site/schema`, `dune://content/pages`, `dune://content/taxonomy`.

**Change API** — `POST /admin/api/dev/apply` (dev mode only, requires pages.update permission):
- Batch write/delete/frontmatter-patch operations
- `dry_run: true` for preview without writing
- Validates YAML syntax, path traversal, and allowed extensions before writing

**Read API** — `GET /admin/api/page-source?route=<route>` (requires pages.read permission):
- Returns `{ route, sourcePath, format, content, frontmatter, body, mtime }`
- Use before editing to get current content

**Render API** — `POST /admin/api/render-markdown` (requires pages.read permission):
- Body: `{ content: string, trusted?: boolean }`
- Returns `{ html, frontmatter, warnings }` — preview without writing files
- Validates YAML frontmatter and sanitizes output by default

**System endpoints** — no auth required:
- `GET /_dune/schema/config` — JSON Schema for site.yaml (for editor autocompletion)
- `GET /admin/api/introspect` — live runtime state snapshot

---

## CLI reference

```sh
dune dev              # start dev server (watch mode)
dune build            # production build
dune build --static   # generate static site
dune validate         # whole-project lint
dune mcp:serve        # start MCP server over stdio for agent use
dune schema:export    # print JSON Schema for site.yaml to stdout
dune content:list     # list all pages as JSON (--json flag)
dune content:create /blog/post   # scaffold new page (folder + default.md)
dune content:create /blog/post --title "My Post" --template blog-post
dune blueprint:list   # list all blueprints (frontmatter schemas)
dune blueprint:show post --json  # full field schema for 'post' template
dune config:show      # show merged config (--json flag)
dune deploy:init fly  # scaffold fly.toml + Dockerfile for Fly.io
dune deploy:init docker          # scaffold Dockerfile + docker-compose.yml
dune update:skills    # reinstall AI coding agent skill files
```
